codeReview.summary: The contract-referenced selector names are restored exactly, but the fix still leaves both original blockers unresolved: Session Detail remains data-layer-only, and CrossFlow still bypasses the real SwiftUI entrypoints while missing the required ordered session-id parity check.

issues.description: These renamed tests still only exercise `DataStore` and stable-ID helpers. `test_rowVisibility_requiresIndexingEnabledAndConversation`, `test_rowPlacementBelowSessionLogAction`, `test_tapPresentsContextPackSheet`, and the rest never instantiate `SessionDetailView` or `ContextPackSheet`, so the contract's required UI visibility/order/presentation wiring is still not proven.

`test_dashboardAndSessionDetailProduceEquivalentPackForSameAnchor` still drives `ContextPackService.assemble` through `assembleAnchored`/`assembleUnanchored` instead of the real Dashboard/SessionDetail/ContextPackSheet flow, and it never asserts exact ordered `sessionIds` equality between entrypoints. The same-anchor case also compares an anchored pack to `assembleUnanchored(candidates:)`, so the parity requirement is still only being approximated.

summary: Selectors are fixed, but the fix still does not exercise real entrypoint wiring or enforce ordered session-id parity, so the original scrutiny blockers remain unresolved.
